fields.description:
## Context
Split from S4 (rebar:6673). The reconciler's backend-neutral core still reached the Jira vendor field MAPPER directly after the epic landed: outbound_differ + apply_inbound + reconcile_check import adapters/jira/outbound_fields; inbound_differ imports inbound_fields; and outbound_links held the Jira link map. This routes the core MAPPER access through the Backend port via dependency injection (ADR 0035 §(d)).

## Delivered (mapper entrypoint injected via the port)
- outbound_differ.compute_outbound_mutations: injected outbound_mapper (Backend-port OutboundMapper), calls map_local_to_remote(); module-level _map_local_to_jira_fields import dropped.
- inbound_differ.compute_inbound_mutations: injected inbound_mapper, calls map_remote_to_local(); direct _map_jira_to_local_fields import dropped.
- apply_inbound._build_hard_delete_recreate: resolves the mapper lazily via select_backend(load_config()).outbound.
- run_differs: production injection point — obtains the configured Backend once and threads backend.outbound / backend.inbound into both differ phases. Injected params default to None, resolving via the neutral select_backend seam (direct differ-test call sites behave identically).
- outbound_links._RELATION_TO_JIRA_LINK relocated into adapters/jira/jira_fields.py.

## Scope boundary (documented residual)
Only the vendor MAPPER entrypoint is routed through the port. Non-mapping helpers with NO port equivalent stay directly imported by the differ (outbound_fields' _diff_fields/_extract_jira_field/_assignee_matches and inbound value maps), and reconcile_check keeps its lazy internal-helper _load_sibling. These operate on the vendor-shaped snapshot and have no Backend-port method; fully relocating them (or extending the port) is a smaller separate follow-up. The neutrality gate documents this boundary explicitly.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] outbound_differ.py, inbound_differ.py, apply_inbound.py have NO module-level import of the vendor MAPPER entrypoint (_map_local_to_jira_fields / _map_jira_to_local_fields); they receive it via Backend-port injection — verified by the AST assertions in tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/test_backend_neutrality.py.
- [ ] The injected mapper is actually USED by each differ (not bypassed) — verified by positive injection tests in the neutrality gate (a passed-in fake mapper is exercised).
- [ ] outbound_links._RELATION_TO_JIRA_LINK is single-sourced in adapters/jira/ (jira_fields.py) and imported from there.
- [ ] Behaviour-preserving: full reconcile unit+integration suite green; whole test tree still collects cleanly.
- [ ] make lint && make typecheck && make test green.
- [Documented residual, not this ticket] Relocating the non-mapping outbound_fields helpers + reconcile_check's lazy load off the core (or onto the port) is a smaller follow-up.
